specialty: AR (mechanical) — primary focus is independent auto repair shops; some collision exposure but not a CR specialist [uncertain]
base_monthly_low_usd: [uncertain] — no published low-tier price. Industry-standard floor for full-service AR agencies is $1,500–$2,000/mo; SMP positions as premium done-for-you, so realistic floor is likely $2,000+
base_monthly_high_usd: [uncertain] — top tier likely $5,000–$8,000+/mo for full done-for-you (SEO + ads + social + content + video). Not published.
typical_monthly_usd: [uncertain] ~$2,500–$5,000/mo for a single-location shop based on industry mid-market positioning and competitor benchmarks. SMP publishes a 'marketing investment' guide recommending 7–10% of gross revenue but does not list package prices.
setup_fee_usd: [uncertain] — not published. Custom-website builds in this segment commonly run $2,000–$5,000 one-time; SMP often bundles into onboarding.
min_ad_spend_usd: [uncertain] — not published. SMP's own content recommends $500–$1,500/mo Google Ads to start.
contract_length_months: [uncertain] — not published. SMP markets itself as a 'trusted advisor' partnership; competitor norm is 6–12 months for done-for-you AR shops.
asset_ownership: [uncertain] — not published. Operating company 5 Stones Media builds custom WordPress sites; ownership terms not disclosed publicly.
pricing_transparency: Quote-only. No pricing on website. Published a long-form 'What Auto Repair Shops Should Expect to Invest in Marketing' blog (Dec 2025) explaining budget frameworks (7–10% of revenue) but no SKU prices.
known_pain_points: [uncertain] — only 23 Glassdoor reviews and no significant public client complaints surfaced. Brian Walker (CEO) actively responds to reviews, suggesting reputation management is tight.
Pricing opacity makes it hard for prospects to compare without a discovery call.
notable_clients: [uncertain] — agency reports 75+ automotive clients (per Agency Analytics case study) but does not publish a public client roster. Podcast features industry guests rather than client logos.
pros: Founders (Brian and Kim Walker) are former independent shop owners — deep domain credibility
Strong inbound brand via the 'Auto Repair Marketing Podcast' (200+ episodes)
Active in industry events (ASTA, ASTE, Vision, AAPEX)
Tailored / boutique approach rather than templated packages
Operating company 5 Stones Media gives them in-house dev + design capacity
cons: No published pricing — friction for budget-conscious buyers
Smaller team (~23) than national competitors; capacity constraints possible
Stronger in mechanical AR than collision — limited public collision case studies
Louisiana-based; Wisconsin / Midwest client base not publicly demonstrated
koffman_relevance: Plausible upgrade vs Hibu (Hibu has 6-month contracts, widespread BBB/Trustpilot complaints about lock-in, deliverables, and account management; SMP positions explicitly as the anti-Hibu trusted-advisor model). Vs Turnkey Marketing: SMP is more digital/content/podcast-led and brand-building, Turnkey is more direct-mail + retention-funnel oriented — they solve different problems. For Koffman (single-location WI, AR + collision), SMP is a credible Hibu replacement if Koffman can stomach $2.5K–$5K/mo and a quote-only process, but the lack of a collision-heavy portfolio is a real gap vs a CR-specialist. Recommend a discovery call to get an actual quote and ask for 3+ collision-shop references before signing.
